description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular, industrial-grade electronic device, likely used for urban infrastructure or utility management. It features a sturdy metal housing with two large, circular, black components protruding from one side, which may be access ports or sensor covers. The top surface is equipped with a vertical antenna and a cylindrical protrusion, suggesting wireless communication or telemetry capabilities. One side displays a set of printed labels and a numeric indicator, possibly for identification or operational status. The overall finish is matte gray, with some areas showing technical markings and a recessed handle for access or maintenance. Its robust construction and utilitarian design indicate it is intended for outdoor or semi-exposed urban environments.
description: A tall, rectangular, gray electronic utility box with two large black ports, an antenna, and technical markings, designed for urban infrastructure.
description_view_0: A side view showing the plain, flat surface of the box with the antenna and cylindrical protrusion visible on top.
description_view_1: This view highlights the two large, black, circular ports protruding from the side, as well as the top-mounted antenna and protrusion.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the two black ports head-on, along with technical labels and identification markings.
description_view_3: This side view shows the printed technical information, a numeric indicator, and a recessed handle, with the ports visible on the opposite side.
